subject: skl_canvas-design-src-https-github-com-anthropics-skil-fe815c69-s-04f79b56-skills-canvas-design-skill-md_fe815c69
evidence: Canvas-design analysis: 'This skill is the static-design counterpart to the algorithmic-art skill, sharing the same philosophy-then-execute structure.' 'The agent first invents a named design movement manifesto, then renders it as a PDF or PNG.' Output is 'a single-page (or multi-page, if requested) visual artifact where text is sparse and design carries the meaning.' ||| Algorithmic-art analysis: 'This skill teaches an AI agent to generate interactive p5.js generative art through a two-phase creative pipeline: first, write a poetic algorithmic philosophy manifesto, then implement it as a living, parameterized visual system.' Output is 'a self-contained HTML artifact — seeded, tunable, reproducible — that works in any browser.'

subject: skl_cellxgene-census-src-https-github-com-k-dense-ai-s-7d8fdd5f-bdb6d39-skills-cellxgene-census-skill-md_7d8fdd5f
evidence: cellxgene-census analysis: 'get_anndata() with var_value_filter and obs_value_filter' — the primary query method returns AnnData objects directly, and the skill 'hands off to anndata and scanpy for downstream analysis.' anndata analysis: teaches 'how to load, filter, normalize, compute PCA, find neighbors, run UMAP, cluster, and identify marker genes' — exactly the manipulation lifecycle for objects cellxgene-census produces. → chains_with, weight 0.92.

subject: skl_anndata-src-https-github-com-k-dense-ai-scientific-96039759-skills-5bdb6d39-skills-anndata-skill-md_96039759
evidence: anndata analysis: teaches that 'normalized data belongs in .X' and how to 'subset and filter' — producing a clean, normalized expression matrix with gene names in .var.index. arboreto analysis: 'The skill assumes a genes-by-cells (or genes-by-samples) matrix with gene names as the index' and notes Arboreto 'is the computational engine behind the widely-used SCENIC pipeline' for single-cell GRN inference. → chains_with, weight 0.78 (handoff requires extracting .X as a DataFrame but the fit is natural and the formats match directly).

subject: skl_bulk-rnaseq-src-https-github-com-k-dense-ai-scient-866022f1-lls-5bdb6d39-skills-bulk-rnaseq-skill-md_866022f1
evidence: bulk-rnaseq analysis: the pipeline produces gene-level expression count matrices via 'featureCounts/HTSeq, and DESeq2' — the standard output is a genes × samples expression matrix. arboreto analysis: 'taking expression matrices and spitting out transcription-factor-to-target-gene confidence scores' and explicitly accepts 'genes-by-samples matrix with gene names as the index.' The featureCounts-style output maps directly to Arboreto's expected input format. → chains_with, weight 0.80.

subject: skl_dask-src-https-github-com-k-dense-ai-scientific-ag-24a35b5e-ent-skills-5bdb6d39-skills-dask-skill-md_24a35b5e
evidence: dask analysis: teaches 'scaling Python data workflows beyond RAM' with 'lazy task graphs, cluster deployment across Kubernetes/SLURM/Yarn, scheduler monitoring, failure recovery.' arboreto analysis: 'it also touches on Dask-distributed runs for larger datasets' but warns 'Running GRNBoost2 on a full transcriptome matrix without filtering can spawn Dask workers that consume all available memory and CPU' — arboreto invokes Dask but provides no Dask operational knowledge. dask fills that gap: it teaches the cluster management, resource monitoring, and failure recovery that arboreto's Dask mode requires but does not explain. → strengthens, weight 0.82.

subject: skl_content-strategy-src-https-github-com-coreyhaines3-980529e8-f920698-skills-content-strategy-skill-md_980529e8
evidence: content-strategy analysis: 'Each source gets a specific extraction method: extract questions asked → FAQ content, extract pain points → problems in their own words'. copywriting analysis: 'The context-gathering section asks the right questions but doesn't help when answers are thin. It assumes someone — the user or a product-marketing context file — knows who the customer is, what they care about, and what language they use. Without rich voice-of-customer input, the copy will be generic regardless of skill quality.' → chains_with, weight 0.82. Content-strategy extracts customer language, pain points, and competitive positioning from research inputs; copywriting consumes those exact inputs as its brief to produce non-generic web page copy. Content-strategy produces the research foundation that copywriting requires to avoid its primary failure mode of generic output.

subject: skl_copywriting-src-https-github-com-coreyhaines31-mar-42797825-nes-ef920698-skills-copywriting-skill-md_42797825
evidence: copywriting analysis: 'There's no treatment of how copy performs when read by AI summarizers, snippet extractors, or voice assistants. A sibling skill (ai-seo) exists for that, but the assumption that web copy is read by humans is increasingly incomplete.' ai-seo analysis: 'This skill teaches agents how to optimize content so AI systems — ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews, Claude, Gemini, Copilot — cite it in their answers.' 'The skill walks them through an audit template, then prescribes concrete structural and authority changes with expected visibility boosts per method.' → chains_with, weight 0.82. Copywriting produces human-readable persuasive web copy; ai-seo takes that copy as input and applies structural patterns (answer blocks, comparison tables, llms.txt creation) to make it extractable and citable by LLMs. The copywriting analysis explicitly names ai-seo as the skill that handles AI-consumed copy.

subject: skl_cro-src-https-github-com-coreyhaines31-marketingsk-e9567f9f-orey-haines-ef920698-skills-cro-skill-md_e9567f9f
evidence: CRO analysis: 'The primary risk is producing advice that sounds specific but isn't. Add social proof near the CTA is actionable enough to implement but not specific enough to work — add what social proof? The skill provides the what-to-check framework but not the how-to-decide.' Copywriting analysis: 'The page-structure framework (headline → social proof → problem → solution → how it works → objections → final CTA) is standard persuasion architecture.' 'The output format ensures the human gets not just copy but reasoning for every choice plus alternatives.' → strengthens, weight 0.75. CRO identifies which page sections are underperforming (missing social proof, weak headlines, poor CTAs) but lacks the specificity to fix them; copywriting provides the structured section-by-section templates and persuasion architecture to rewrite those sections with concrete, implementable copy. CRO's 'Copy Alternatives' output category is fulfilled by copywriting's annotated copy generation.

subject: skl_azure-ai-voicelive-dotnet-src-https-github-com-mic-dface443-kills-azure-ai-voicelive-dotnet-skill-md_dface443
evidence: VoiceLive .NET analysis: 'Teaches .NET developers real-time voice-to-voice AI using Azure OpenAI's VoiceLive service layered on Cognitive Services Speech SDK. Streaming audio pipelines, voice model config, conversation state, error handling.' VoiceLive Java analysis: 'Teaches wiring up real-time voice conversations using Azure's AI VoiceLive Java SDK — connecting streaming audio to Azure OpenAI models, configuring voice profiles, handling bi-directional sessions.' → alternatives, weight 0.90. Both skills solve the identical core task — real-time voice-to-voice AI via Azure VoiceLive SDK — but target mutually exclusive language ecosystems (.NET/C# vs Java/JVM). A pack should choose one based on the project's language stack.

subject: skl_azure-ai-projects-java-src-https-github-com-micros-451e994c-a-skills-azure-ai-projects-java-skill-md_451e994c
evidence: Projects analysis: 'creating projects, wiring data connections, registering models, deploying artifacts. Pure infrastructure scaffolding.' Agents analysis: 'create agent with model/instructions/tools, async task management, server-side conversation state. Pick only if team map already pinned to Azure Foundry.' → chains_with, weight 0.73. Projects provisions AI Foundry infrastructure and registers model deployments; Agents consumes those provisioned models to create and run persistent AI agents on Azure Foundry. The chain is sequential: Projects provisions → Agents runs. Both share Java and Azure Foundry, making the handoff natural. The connection requires the minor inference that the models Projects registers are the same models Agents instantiates agents against, but no explicit handoff is described in either analysis.

subject: skl_cloud-solution-architect-src-https-github-com-micr-fdee9a93-skills-cloud-solution-architect-skill-md_fdee9a93
evidence: cloud-solution-architect analysis: 'The agent loads this skill and walks through the 7-step review workflow: it picks an architecture style based on domain complexity and team autonomy, selects Azure services using the technology choice decision framework, applies relevant design patterns… validates against all five WAF pillars, and produces an Architecture Decision Record.' azure-cloud-migrate analysis: 'moving on-premises into Azure via Microsoft-prescribed lifecycle: discovery/assessment with Azure Migrate, wave planning, execution (lift-and-shift, re-platform, refactor), landing zone config, cost estimation, post-migration validation.' → chains_with, weight 0.73. The architect designs the target Azure architecture (service selections, WAF-validated patterns, ADRs) that the migration skill needs as input for landing zone configuration and workload placement decisions. Requires inference that greenfield designs translate to migration target designs, but service selection, landing zone patterns, and WAF compliance directly inform migration execution steps.

subject: skl_mcp-builder-src-https-github-com-microsoft-skills-d6d41f0c-423e-github-skills-mcp-builder-skill-md_d6d41f0c
evidence: mcp-builder (Microsoft) analysis: 'four-phase workflow guide for building MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers. It covers planning, implementation in three languages (TypeScript, Python, C#), testing, and creating evaluation question sets… deeply ties into Microsoft's own MCP server ecosystem, spending considerable real estate on Azure MCP, Foundry MCP, and Fabric MCP before you've written a single line of code.' mcp-builder (Anthropic) analysis: 'structured, four-phase development guide for building MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers… What distinguishes this skill is Phase 1's design philosophy: the tradeoff between comprehensive API coverage and workflow tools, naming conventions for discoverability, context management… Phase 4 — the evaluation system — is the most distinctive piece. The skill requires you to create 10 complex, read-only, multi-step questions that test whether an LLM can actually accomplish real tasks with your server.' → alternatives, weight 0.85. Both skills solve the same core task — guiding developers through building MCP servers — with the same four-phase structure (plan, implement, test, evaluate). The Microsoft version adds deep Azure ecosystem binding (48-service Azure MCP catalog, C#/.NET patterns, Entra ID auth, Foundry/Fabric) while the Anthropic version adds tool design philosophy depth and a rigorous evaluation framework. Pack synthesis would choose one based on platform: Microsoft ecosystem → MS version; general/TypeScript → Anthropic version.

subject: skl_skill-creator-src-https-github-com-microsoft-skill-f5dab2cc-3e-github-skills-skill-creator-skill-md_f5dab2cc
evidence: skill-creator (Microsoft) analysis: 'meta-skill: its job is to teach an AI agent how to create other skills, specifically Azure SDK wrapper skills conforming to Microsoft's internal skill repository conventions… a deeply institutional document encoding Microsoft's Azure authentication policies, directory layouts, testing harness expectations, and documentation update procedures. If you are inside Microsoft's microsoft/skills repo adding an Azure SDK skill, this is your required playbook. If you are anywhere else, most of this document is irrelevant noise.' skill-creator (Anthropic) analysis: 'meta-skill: a full development lifecycle for skill authoring, covering everything from initial intent capture through parallel test-case evaluation, benchmark scoring, blind A/B comparison, and description optimization for trigger accuracy… The core innovation is the parallel evaluation system: for each test case, the skill instructs the agent to spawn two subagents simultaneously — one with the skill loaded, one without — then grade outputs against assertions, compute benchmark stats… This is the single most ambitious skill-authoring guide I've seen.' → alternatives, weight 0.88. Both skills solve the same core task — teaching agents how to create skills (meta-skill authoring). The Microsoft version is a compliance checklist narrowly targeting Azure SDK skills within Microsoft's specific repo infrastructure (symlinks, pnpm harness, docs-site extraction, verbatim auth callout blocks). The Anthropic version is a general-purpose skill engineering framework with automated evaluation loops, benchmark scoring, and description optimization. Same task identity, fundamentally different scope, methodology, and audience. Pack synthesis would choose one based on context: inside Microsoft's skills repo → MS version; anywhere else → Anthropic version.

subject: skl_competitor-analysis-src-https-github-com-phuryn-pm-ece2d0e0-arch-skills-competitor-analysis-skill-md_ece2d0e0
evidence: competitor-analysis analysis: 'identify 5 direct competitors, then profiles each one across a fixed template: market position, core strengths, weaknesses, business model, competitive threats, and differentiation opportunities.' ansoff-matrix analysis: 'Skip it when you have no data or when the question isn't about growth expansion. Biggest risk is producing strategy theater — well-structured analysis based on guesses. Biggest benefit is the risk-calibrated quadrant specificity...Earns a spot in a tight catalog as a solid strategy-analysis tool, provided it's loaded alongside skills that supply the market data it needs.' → chains_with, weight 0.82. The ansoff-matrix analysis explicitly warns it needs skills that supply market data — competitor-analysis produces exactly that: competitive landscape profiles with market position, strengths, weaknesses, and differentiation data that ground Ansoff's quadrant analysis in reality rather than speculation.

subject: skl_business-model-src-https-github-com-phuryn-pm-skil-58f416c0-strategy-skills-business-model-skill-md_58f416c0
evidence: business-model analysis: 'walks through all 9 building blocks (Key Partners, Key Activities, Key Resources, Value Propositions, Customer Relationships, Channels, Customer Segments, Cost Structure, Revenue Streams) using the Strategyzer/Alexander Osterwalder framework.' ansoff-matrix analysis: 'The skill assumes the company has a clearly defined "current market" and "current product." For early-stage startups pivoting every quarter, or multi-product enterprises where "current product" spans fifty SKUs, this boundary blurs and the quadrant analysis loses precision.' → chains_with, weight 0.72. BMC documents the current-state baseline — customer segments (market) and value propositions (product) — that Ansoff assumes as its starting point. Without BMC, Ansoff's quadrant mapping lacks a defined origin. The handoff is conceptual rather than format-specific: BMC produces structured documentation of the as-is business, and Ansoff builds growth strategy on that foundation.

subject: skl_create-prd-src-https-github-com-phuryn-pm-skills-p-152f8b28-pm-execution-skills-create-prd-skill-md_152f8b28
evidence: create-prd analysis: 'an 8-section PRD template wrapped in agent instructions: gather information, think step-by-step, fill in the template, save the file.' release-notes analysis: 'A structured translator that consumes technical source material — JIRA exports, Linear tickets, PRDs, git logs — and produces polished user-facing release notes.' → chains_with, weight 0.85. release-notes explicitly lists PRDs as a primary source material type. create-prd produces a PRD markdown file as its output. The format match is direct: a PRD contains the feature descriptions, acceptance criteria, and scope context that release-notes transforms into user-facing language. The handoff requires no format conversion.

subject: skl_analyze-feature-requests-src-https-github-com-phur-80ef1368-skills-analyze-feature-requests-skill-md_80ef1368
evidence: analyze-feature-requests analysis: 'categorize requests into themes, assess strategic alignment against stated goals, prioritize the top 3 by impact/effort/risk/alignment...the PM walks away with a prioritized, testable shortlist and a clear rationale for each choice.' outcome-roadmap analysis: 'rewrites feature-list roadmap items into outcome statements following the "Enable [segment] to [outcome] so that [business impact]" format.' → chains_with, weight 0.75. analyze-feature-requests produces a prioritized feature shortlist; outcome-roadmap takes feature-list items as input and transforms them into outcome language. The handoff requires the PM to promote analyzed features into roadmap commitments — a standard sequential step in product planning that bridges the two skills' natural input/output formats.

subject: skl_create-prd-src-https-github-com-phuryn-pm-skills-p-152f8b28-pm-execution-skills-create-prd-skill-md_152f8b28
evidence: Phuryn create-prd analysis: 'an 8-section PRD template wrapped in agent instructions...What little methodology exists is surface-level...no examples of what good looks like, no anti-patterns, no quality gates, no review checklist...This is a generic PRD template. You could swap it with any other PRD template and get roughly the same result.' PoP deliver-prd analysis: 'a structured workflow for generating Product Requirements Documents...an eight-step process (summarize problem, define goals/metrics, outline solution, detail functional requirements, set scope boundaries, address technical considerations, identify dependencies/risks, propose timeline) and a quality checklist...the explicit "when NOT to use" section that routes you to sibling skills.' → alternatives, weight 0.85. Both solve the same core task — generating a PRD document. Phuryn offers a bare template with no methodology; PoP offers a structured workflow with quality gates and routing logic. A pack would choose one based on whether it needs lightweight formatting (Phuryn) or methodology-grounded production with preconditions (PoP).

subject: skl_competitor-analysis-src-https-github-com-phuryn-pm-ece2d0e0-arch-skills-competitor-analysis-skill-md_ece2d0e0
evidence: Phuryn competitor-analysis analysis: 'Compared to discover-competitive-analysis, this skill leans harder on the agent's web search capability...but provides less structural discipline: no confidence-level documentation requirement, no positioning matrix construction method, no quality checklist, no cross-skill routing guidance. It's a prompt with a long output template attached.' PoP discover-competitive-analysis analysis: 'a 7-step workflow, a detailed output template with 11 mandatory sections, a quality checklist, a "when NOT to use" section that cross-references sibling skills, and explicit confidence-level documentation requirements...the 2x2 positioning matrix, the feature comparison grid, the strengths-weaknesses deep dives.' → alternatives, weight 0.90. Both skills perform competitive landscape analysis with competitor profiling and strategic recommendations as their core task. The Phuryn analysis itself explicitly identifies discover-competitive-analysis as the comparator skill, noting the missing quality infrastructure. PoP offers more structural rigor (confidence notation, quality checklist, 2x2 matrix, cross-skill routing) while Phuryn offers a simpler web-search-driven approach with a mandatory 5-competitor scope. A pack would choose one.

subject: skl_define-problem-statement-src-https-github-com-prod-93d7d595-skills-define-problem-statement-skill-md_93d7d595
evidence: define-problem-statement analysis: 'the 'when NOT to use' section is genuinely instructive: if the problem is already agreed, go to deliver-prd' and 'it is the entry point that every other skill in the suite either builds on or routes back to.' ||| deliver-prd analysis: 'it explicitly redirects you to define-problem-statement when the problem is unframed' and 'A solved problem and aligned solution already exist' (assumption #1).

subject: skl_deliver-prd-src-https-github-com-product-on-purpos-f3cad5c7-ose-f2ce15e5-skills-deliver-prd-skill-md_f3cad5c7
evidence: deliver-prd analysis: routing includes 'to deliver-user-stories when you just need tickets.' ||| deliver-user-stories analysis: 'Step 1 says Review the PRD or feature description' and 'the skill's effectiveness depends on the quality of the upstream PRD.' It also routes to deliver-prd in 'when NOT to use' when the feature is not yet specified.

subject: skl_define-hypothesis-src-https-github-com-product-on-fb04a2c3-ce15e5-skills-define-hypothesis-skill-md_fb04a2c3
evidence: define-hypothesis analysis: 'a six-step process: state the belief... quality checklist enforces falsifiability' and produces testable, quantified hypotheses about user behavior and outcomes. ||| define-problem-statement analysis: 'if the "problem" is actually an unverified assumption, frame it as a define-hypothesis first.' The routing from problem-statement explicitly directs users to hypothesis formation when the problem framing is untested.

subject: skl_discover-interview-synthesis-src-https-github-com-ef79db60-ls-discover-interview-synthesis-skill-md_ef79db60
evidence: discover-interview-synthesis analysis: 'When NOT to Use: synthesized findings ready for framing → problem-statement.' Produces themes, quotes, and insight statements backed by participant evidence. ||| define-problem-statement analysis: Step 2 'Look for evidence from user research, support tickets, or behavioral data.' The skill explicitly expects user research evidence as input for pain-point exploration.

subject: skl_define-hypothesis-src-https-github-com-product-on-fb04a2c3-ce15e5-skills-define-hypothesis-skill-md_fb04a2c3
evidence: define-hypothesis analysis: hidden assumption #3 — 'This skill does not support comparison or prioritization across hypotheses. Product work often involves comparing multiple competing hypotheses about the same problem space.' ||| define-prioritization-framework analysis: 'the skill explicitly says this ("this skill ranks a list, it does not discover what belongs on it") and cross-references define-opportunity-tree for list generation.' The cross-skill composition section references define-hypothesis among the upstream skills that feed into it.

subject: skl_discover-interview-synthesis-src-https-github-com-ef79db60-ls-discover-interview-synthesis-skill-md_ef79db60
evidence: discover-interview-synthesis analysis: produces structured research findings — themes backed by 3+ participants, verbatim quotes, insight statements that explain 'why not just what,' and recommendations tied to evidence. Pushes beyond observation to actionable synthesis. ||| define-prioritization-framework analysis: 'Kano is gated behind customer research' and 'the skill will flatly refuse... Kano without customer research.' The applicability filter gates Kano behind the presence of customer research evidence.

subject: skl_foundation-persona-src-https-github-com-product-on-80fcbbb2-e15e5-skills-foundation-persona-skill-md_80fcbbb2
evidence: Persona analysis: 'A product team has completed a round of user interviews... and needs a persona to anchor an upcoming PRD and stakeholder presentation. You load this skill in product mode, feed it the interview synthesis, and it fills the template with evidence-linked claims.' Journey map analysis: 'The skill will reject five specific inadequate inputs: no persona or scope' and 'You have a completed interview synthesis from 12 users, survey data from 200 respondents, and a defined persona.' → chains_with, weight 0.85: persona produces the defined persona artifact that journey map explicitly requires as a prerequisite input. Without a persona, journey map refuses or downgrades to hypothesis mode.

subject: skl_discover-stakeholder-summary-src-https-github-com-e568212c-ls-discover-stakeholder-summary-skill-md_e568212c
evidence: Stakeholder summary analysis: 'plan communication' (step 6 of 7), 'plan communication cadence and channels for high-priority stakeholders,' and 'routes users to... foundation-stakeholder-update' when you need to actually communicate. Stakeholder update analysis: 'infers the right channel and audience variant' using generic heuristics: 'engineering/design → Slack, leadership → email, mixed → Notion. This is reasonable but not universal.' → strengthens, weight 0.78: stakeholder summary's communication plan captures actual per-stakeholder channel preferences based on organizational context. This replaces stakeholder update's generic heuristic channel inference with evidence-based channel selection, improving accuracy of the output communication.

subject: skl_define-problem-statement-src-https-github-com-prod-93d7d595-skills-define-problem-statement-skill-md_93d7d595
evidence: Problem statement analysis: 'the entry point that every other skill in the suite either builds on or routes back to' and 'The output follows an external template with sections for Problem Summary, User Impact, Business Context, Success Criteria, Constraints, and Open Questions.' Stakeholder briefings analysis: 'takes any source artifact (PRD, research, GTM plan, experiment results, retro) and produces a canonical master document with numbered, traceable claims' and 'You have just finished a PRD or a major discovery synthesis. It's dense, technical, and 40 pages. Engineering needs the architecture decisions and tradeoffs. The executive sponsor needs the what, why, and investment ask in one page.' → chains_with, weight 0.85: problem statement produces a structured discovery artifact with quantified user impact, business context, and success criteria. Stakeholder briefings consumes this as a source artifact and projects audience-tailored briefings — executive gets problem summary and investment case, engineering gets constraints and success criteria, PMM gets user impact context.

subject: skl_discover-competitive-analysis-src-https-github-com-8688ef32-s-discover-competitive-analysis-skill-md_8688ef32
evidence: Competitive analysis analysis: 'routes the user to discover-market-sizing for TAM questions' and produces 'feature matrix building, 2x2 positioning mapping, and strategic recommendation generation for 3-5 competitors' including pricing comparisons and market positioning data. Market sizing analysis: uses 'comparable company' as one of four frameworks, runs 'multiple frameworks (top-down, bottom-up, comparable company, analogous market), compares where they converge and diverge,' and needs reliable competitor revenue proxies and market-share estimates. → strengthens, weight 0.72: competitive analysis output provides structured competitor intelligence (pricing, positioning, feature coverage) that market sizing's comparable-company framework needs. This reduces the agent's reliance on ad-hoc web search for competitor data during market sizing and improves the quality of revenue-proxy estimates. The chain requires moderate inference — market sizing analysis does not explicitly name competitive analysis as input, but the comparable-company framework inherently depends on the kind of competitor data competitive analysis produces.

subject: skl_brand-dna-src-https-github-com-thatrebeccarae-clau-8ef6020e-arton-8a108923-skills-brand-dna-skill-md_8ef6020e
evidence: Brand DNA analysis: 'The five-axis voice scoring (formal↔casual, expert↔accessible, bold↔subtle, rational↔emotional, traditional↔innovative) with explicit signal-to-score mapping adds precision that "write in the brand voice" prompts lack' and 'designed as machine input for downstream content-creation skills.' Copywriting Frameworks analysis: 'The biggest unstated assumption is that the agent already knows enough about the product, market, and audience to fill the templates with specific, credible content. The frameworks provide structure, not substance... This is fine if the agent arrives with context from a brand-dna or customer-research skill — but this skill doesn't declare that dependency.' → Brand DNA produces the structured brand data (voice scores, values, audience descriptors) that Copywriting Frameworks needs to fill its templates with non-generic content. CF's analysis explicitly names brand-dna as the missing dependency. chains_with, weight 0.88.

subject: skl_brand-dna-src-https-github-com-thatrebeccarae-clau-8ef6020e-arton-8a108923-skills-brand-dna-skill-md_8ef6020e
evidence: Landing Page Optimizer analysis: 'The skill will recommend a neon-orange CTA button on a brand that uses muted earth tones, and the recommendation will be correct for conversion and wrong for brand consistency. The skill provides no framework for resolving that tension.' Brand DNA analysis: 'The CSS parsing rules are sensible but fragile... extracts colors and fonts to build a structured brand-profile.json that other skills can consume programmatically — it treats brand identity as a data contract, not a vibe check.' → Brand DNA can supply the extracted color palette and brand constraints that Landing Page Optimizer lacks, preventing LPO from making conversion-first recommendations that violate brand identity. LPO's analysis explicitly identifies brand-consistency violation as a real risk. strengthens, weight 0.75.

subject: skl_copywriting-frameworks-src-https-github-com-thatre-88656ba3-3-skills-copywriting-frameworks-skill-md_88656ba3
evidence: Facebook Ads analysis: 'The skill is almost entirely audit-and-diagnosis oriented. It has no campaign builder workflow, no ad copy generator templates, no creative brief framework. If you ask it to build something from scratch, it will give you the campaign structure diagram and say "good luck."' Copywriting Frameworks analysis: 'The framework-selection guide — a lookup table mapping specific situations (short ad copy, landing page hero, email sequence, cold outreach) to the best framework with a one-line rationale... The CTA anti-patterns section... Banning "Submit," "Click Here," and context-free "Sign Up" gives an agent concrete editing rules.' → Copywriting Frameworks fills the ad copy generation gap that Facebook Ads analysis explicitly identifies. FB diagnoses campaigns and structures them; CF provides the actual persuasive copy to populate ad creative. strengthens, weight 0.78.

subject: skl_copywriting-frameworks-src-https-github-com-thatre-88656ba3-3-skills-copywriting-frameworks-skill-md_88656ba3
evidence: TikTok Ads analysis: 'The skill will confidently describe the creative formula — hook, story, CTA, 9:16 vertical, sound on, cut every 2-3 seconds — but has zero guidance on how to actually produce that creative. There's no script template, no brief format, no guidance on working with creators.' Copywriting Frameworks analysis: 'The framework-selection guide — a lookup table mapping specific situations to the best framework — turns a reference into a decision tool. Each framework gets a worked example in a realistic SaaS/marketing-tech context, so the agent sees the template instantiated, not just described.' → Copywriting Frameworks provides the script structure and headline formulas that TikTok Ads lacks for actually writing ad creative. TK describes the creative formula; CF provides the frameworks to execute it. strengthens, weight 0.75.

subject: skl_copywriting-frameworks-src-https-github-com-thatre-88656ba3-3-skills-copywriting-frameworks-skill-md_88656ba3
evidence: Landing Page Optimizer analysis: 'A non-marketer... can paste the page content or describe the layout to the agent, and this skill will produce a structured audit comparing every section against best-practice patterns. The agent will flag a headline that describes the product instead of the outcome, a CTA button that says "Submit" instead of an action verb, a form with 11 fields... The user walks away with 8-12 concrete changes to make.' Copywriting Frameworks analysis: 'The CTA anti-patterns section is the second genuinely useful piece. Banning "Submit," "Click Here," and context-free "Sign Up" gives an agent concrete editing rules rather than vague "write better CTAs" advice... The headline formulas section provides eight headline formulas for structuring headlines that grab attention.' → LPO diagnoses what's broken in landing page copy and CTAs; CF provides the specific frameworks and rules to rewrite them. LPO's pattern-matching identifies problems, CF's templates solve them. strengthens, weight 0.82.

subject: skl_copywriting-frameworks-src-https-github-com-thatre-88656ba3-3-skills-copywriting-frameworks-skill-md_88656ba3
evidence: Google Ads analysis: 'It is reference-heavy rather than workflow-rich... The skill's actual executable surface is the 11-point audit workflow and the hard rules. Everything else is context the agent would need those separate files for.' Copywriting Frameworks analysis: 'The framework-selection guide maps specific situations (short ad copy, landing page hero, email sequence, cold outreach) to the best framework with a one-line rationale... The CTA anti-patterns section... gives an agent concrete editing rules.' → Google Ads skill audits campaign structure, bidding, and tracking but its executable surface does not include ad copy generation. Copywriting Frameworks supplies the copy structures (headlines, descriptions, CTAs) needed for RSAs and other text-based Google Ads formats. The Google Ads analysis does not explicitly flag the lack of copy generation (unlike Facebook Ads), so the inference is moderate but the complement is real. strengthens, weight 0.72.
